heading: What Makes a Good Compression Tool
text: A good image compression tool should balance three things: compression ratio, visual quality, and ease of use. The best tools let you reduce file sizes significantly while keeping images visually indistinguishable from the originals at normal viewing sizes.

heading: Browser-Based Tools
text: ToolPic processes images entirely in your browser using WebAssembly technology. Your files never leave your device, making it the most privacy-friendly option available. It supports JPEG, PNG, WebP, and AVIF formats with adjustable quality settings and batch processing.

heading: Desktop and CLI Tools
text: ImageOptim is a free desktop application for macOS that compresses images using multiple optimization algorithms. It is particularly good at optimizing PNG files and can process entire folders at once. Being a native application, it handles large batches faster than browser-based tools.

heading: Privacy Comparison
text: When choosing a compression tool, consider where your images are being processed. Server-based tools like TinyPNG upload your images to remote servers for processing. While reputable services delete files after processing, your images do briefly exist on third-party infrastructure.
